index.name: Nifty Pharma Index
index.description: Nifty Pharma tracks 20 companies in India — world's 3rd largest pharma market by volume, 14th by value. India's title 'pharmacy of the world': 20% of global generic medicines, 60% of global vaccines. Structural advantages: 60-70% cost discount vs US/Europe enables aggressive pricing for emerging markets and tender wins. Two profit pools: (1) US generics (40% revenue for Sun Pharma, Dr Reddy's, Aurobindo) — each USD generics approval adds ~₹50-100 Cr annual profit; (2) Domestic branded generics (30% revenue) — 50%+ margins, resists price cuts, 15-20% growth. Exports headwind: generic pricing pressure from US competitors and China (world's largest API manufacturer, undercutting costs). Regulatory advantage: Indian companies' FDA-approved facilities number 200+ globally, creating moat vs competitors from other emerging markets. Capex efficiency: INR 1 Cr capex generates $10-15 Cr annual revenue vs global pharma ratio of $1-3 Cr. Volatility drivers: US FDA approvals (binary events creating 20-50% stock moves), complex generics timelines (4-6 years to approval vs simple generics 2 years). Long-term: biosimilars provide 15-20% premiums vs simple generics but require $200-300M capex and regulatory expertise concentrated at Tier-1 companies.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company by market cap (₹3.5 lakh crore). US specialty dermatology leader — Ilumya (psoriasis) and Cequa (dry eyes) are blockbusters. Taro Pharmaceutical in Canada/US. Strong domestic branded generics in 100+ countries.

holdings.name: Dr. Reddy's Labs
holdings.description: Pioneer of India's generics-to-global strategy. $3B+ revenue company. Complex generics and biosimilars (gRevlimid launch). Exceptional API manufacturing with backward integration. Founded by Dr. K. Anji Reddy in 1984 with ₹25 lakh.

holdings.name: Cipla
holdings.description: Making life-saving drugs affordable since 1935. Cipla's HIV drug pricing advocacy saved millions of lives in Africa in the 2000s. Leader in respiratory inhalers (Seroflo, Foracort), anti-infectives and consumer wellness via Cipla Health (Nasivion, Cipladine).

holdings.name: Divis Laboratories
holdings.description: India's top API and nutraceuticals contract manufacturer. Supplies to 9 of world's top 10 global pharma companies. Pristine USFDA compliance record across all plants. Fully backward integrated, debt-free balance sheet and 30%+ EBITDA margins.

holdings.name: Aurobindo Pharma
holdings.description: Major US generics player with 800+ ANDA filings, largest injectables portfolio among Indian pharma. API-to-formulations integration provides ~30% cost advantage. Strong oncology and penicillin segment. Aurovida Formulations for biosimilars.

holdings.name: Lupin Ltd
holdings.description: Specialty generics company. US complex generics include gSpiriva (respiratory) and gFostair. India branded generics in cardiology, CNS, diabetes. Growing biosimilars pipeline — etanercept, adalimumab under development for US and EU.

holdings.name: Abbott India
holdings.description: Indian subsidiary of Abbott Laboratories USA. Premium branded prescription medicines. Thyronorm is India's largest-selling thyroid brand. Cremaffin, Duphaston, Digene are household medicines. Consistent compounder at 20%+ ROCE.

holdings.name: Torrent Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Branded generics powerhouse. India dominance in cardiovascular (Shelcal, Nikoran) and CNS (Nexito). Unichem India acquisition doubled domestic scale. Germany and Brazil branded operations contributing stable international revenue.

holdings.name: Zydus Lifesciences
holdings.description: Ahmedabad-based pharma innovator. Launched ZyCoV-D — world's first DNA-based COVID vaccine. Strong US generics and complex generics (transdermal patches). Lipaglyn — world's first glitazar for diabetes approved in India.

holdings.name: Ipca Laboratories
holdings.description: Specialty API and formulations company. Largest producer of anti-malarial APIs globally. Strong branded generics in cardiovascular and musculoskeletal. Europe business growing rapidly post-compliance improvements.

name: Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund - Kotak Nifty Chemicals ETF

index.name: Nifty Chemicals Index (TRI)
index.description: Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund - Kotak Nifty Chemicals ETF tracks the Nifty Chemicals Index (TRI) index.

holdings.name: Pidilite Industries
holdings.description: Fevicol, M-Seal, Dr. Fixit — India's dominant adhesive and sealant brand. Construction chemicals growing 20%+ with affordable housing boom. 60%+ gross margins from branded consumer adhesives. DIY and professional products both expanding.

holdings.name: SRF Limited
holdings.description: India's #1 fluorochemicals company and global refrigerant gas (R22, R134a) producer. Technical textiles (tyre cord, polyester, nylon) for Bridgestone, Michelin. New fluoropolymers for EV, aerospace and semiconductor applications. CapEx cycle 2022-26.

holdings.name: Deepak Nitrite
holdings.description: Midstream specialty chemicals — nitro-aromatic compounds used in dyes, agrochemicals, rubber chemicals and pharma intermediates. Deepak Phenolics (Dahej) makes phenol and acetone domestically for the first time, replacing Chinese imports.

holdings.name: Navin Fluorine International
holdings.description: CDMO for fluorinated APIs, agro-intermediates and specialty fluorochemicals. Protected mode high-value CRAMS business growing for global innovators. Per PFAS regulations in Europe boosting demand for fluorine-free alternatives NAFL develops.

holdings.name: Aarti Industries
holdings.description: India's largest benzene-based chemical company. Sulfuric acid, nitro-chloro-benzene, nitro-toluene — 100+ specialty chemicals used in dyes, pharma and agrochemicals. China+1 beneficiary as global buyers diversify sourcing from Indian manufacturers.

holdings.name: Gujarat Fluorochemicals
holdings.description: Transitioning from refrigerants to PTFE and other fluoropolymers for EV batteries, semiconductor fabrication and aerospace. India's only PTFE producer + India's first HFO (new-generation low-GWP refrigerant) maker. R&D-intensive expansion.
